key_insight: Hamamatsu S13360-3050PE SiPMs remained operational in CubeSat gamma-ray detectors for more than four years in low Earth orbit, showing they can survive space radiation when adequately shielded.
engineering_relevance: This supports using compact, low-voltage SiPM-based scintillation detectors for long-duration space missions where size, power, and fast response matter.
method: Tracked dark count rate and low-energy threshold over time in flight.
Compared performance across GRBAlpha, GRBBeta, and VZLUSAT-2 CubeSats.
Used CsI(Tl) scintillator detectors with 2.5 mm PbSb shielding.
evidence_signal: Flight data from GRBAlpha over 4+ years showed that shielded SiPMs could be used successfully in LEO despite radiation-driven degradation trends.
watch_item: Radiation damage still increases dark count rate and can raise the low-energy threshold, so mission design must account for shielding, orbit, and expected lifetime.
paper_title: Long-term performance of SiPMs in space environment measured by GRBAlpha, GRBBeta, and VZLUSAT-2 CubeSats
